task: Write a Python function depth_max(s) returning the maximum nesting depth of square brackets in the string, counting only the characters [ and ] and ignoring everything else. When a closing bracket appears before its opener, or any opener is never closed, return -1. An empty string returns 0. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the requirements for correctness, conciseness, and readability.
trace: I approached this task by iterating through each character in the input string. Whenever I encounter an opening square bracket, I increment a depth counter and compare it to the maximum depth found so far. If I encounter a closing square bracket, I first check if there is an active depth before decrementing the depth counter. In case of a closing bracket without its corresponding opener or an opener without a corresponding closing bracket, I return -1. Additionally, I handle the edge case where the input string is empty by setting the maximum depth to 0 and returning it if no characters were processed.
